name: Judd Trail (Jackass Ginger Pool)
tagline: A quick shady loop through Nu'uanu Valley with a hidden swimming hole the locals call Jackass Ginger.
parking: Free roadside parking along Nu'uanu Pali Drive near the trailhead — limited spots
bestTime: Midday when you want a quick shady escape from the heat
talkStory: Just under a mile loop with barely 100 ft elevation change — this is about as easy as it gets. The trail loops through a eucalyptus and Norfolk pine forest along Nu'uanu Stream. Jackass Ginger Pool is a popular mud-sliding and rope-swing spot for local kids, but swim at your own risk (leptospirosis concern in all freshwater). Great for a quick nature break if you're in the area, but don't drive across the island for it.
